assertion_results.text: Migration does NOT use a bare ALTER TABLE RENAME COLUMN as the primary strategy (may mention it as unsafe alternative)
assertion_results.evidence: Migration uses 'ALTER TABLE users RENAME COLUMN email TO email_address' as the sole strategy

assertion_results.text: Migration uses a dual-write or copy-column strategy: adds new column, backfills, migrates application code, then drops old column
assertion_results.evidence: No dual-write strategy. Uses bare RENAME which breaks all references instantly

assertion_results.text: A complete rollback migration is provided that reverses the changes
The migration addresses zero-downtime by ensuring both old and new columns coexist during transition
assertion_results.evidence: Bare RENAME does not allow coexistence — old column name stops working immediately

assertion_results.text: Verification queries are included that confirm data was correctly copied from email to email_address
Data backup commands are included before any destructive operations
A deployment runbook is provided with phased steps (add column, backfill, deploy app changes, drop old column)
The migration mentions or addresses the need to update application code, background jobs, or queries that reference the old column name
assertion_results.evidence: Comment states 'You'll need to update your application code to reference the new column name'
